betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6ME Controller§f:
The ME Controller (appliedenergistics2:controller) is the §ecore block of an AE2 network§f. It defines the network's boundaries, manages connected devices, and serves as the crafting CPU when augmented with Crafting Units.

§6Crafting Recipe (network/blocks/controller.json)§f:
- 1× Engineering Processor (appliedenergistics2:material:17) at center
- 4× Iron Ingot (vanilla, in corners)
- 4× Fluix Crystal (appliedenergistics2:material:7) in cardinal directions
- 1× Quartz Glass (appliedenergistics2:quartz_glass) at center (top/bottom)
- Yields 1× ME Controller

§6Function§f:
The ME Controller:
- §bDefines the network boundary§f: only devices connected via cable to the controller are part of the network
- §bStores the network's power buffer§f: 200 AE internal buffer (same as Energy Acceptor)
- §bManages device discovery§f: scans for connected devices and adds them to the network
- §bActs as the crafting CPU§f: when Crafting Units are attached, handles pattern crafting
- §bProvides the network's power output face§f: emits AE for connected cables

§6Multiblock Structure§f:
The ME Controller can be §ecubed up§f to 7×7×7 for higher performance:
- Single Controller: 1 channel, basic functionality
- 3×3×3 cube: 9 channels, standard for early networks
- 5×5×5 cube: 25 channels, mid-game
- 7×7×7 cube: 49 channels, late-game massive network

The cube must be a solid hollow block of ME Controllers. Adding Crafting Units (any face) increases crafting slots.

§6Direct Descendants of ME Controller (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 1 ingredient item used in the controller recipe:
- Engineering Processor (appliedenergistics2:material:17)

The ME Controller is the §eessential root block§f — every ME network needs at least one.

§6Why It Matters§f:
The ME Controller is the §eheart of the AE2 system§f. Without it:
- No network can be formed
- No devices can communicate
- No items can be stored
- No crafting can occur

§6Setup§f:
1. Place the ME Controller
2. Connect cables to any face
3. Add an Energy Acceptor for power
4. Add devices to the network (terminals, drives, interfaces, etc.)
5. The network forms automatically when at least 1 device is connected

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30149 (Printed Circuits & Processors) — the controller requires an Engineering Processor.

§eTip§f: For most networks, a single ME Controller is sufficient. Only cube up when you need more channels (typically >32 connected devices).

§eTip§f: When cubing up, place Crafting Units on the §eouter faces§f of the cube. This makes the crafting slots more accessible.

§cWarning§f: Breaking the ME Controller breaks the entire network. All connected devices become "offline" and their contents may be lost if storage cells are removed without proper shutdown.
